specialized.note: Next-generation Flippy is in commercial rollout at White Castle with 2025 expansion to Jack in the Box; the unit installs overnight in existing kitchens and replaces the fry station only.

humanoid.note: π0 and π0.5 demos cover food-handling and kitchen-style manipulation that generalizes to unseen environments, but the work is research-stage with no paying counter-service deployment.

occupations.title: Laborers and Freight, Stock, and Material Movers, Hand

specialized.note: UPS announced a $120M order for ~400 Pickle units to autonomously unload trailers, rolling out through 2026–2027; earlier pilots ran with Maersk and DHL.

humanoid.note: Digit passed 100,000 totes moved in live commercial operation at GXO's Spanx fulfillment site by November 2025 under a multi-year robots-as-a-service agreement — the first formal humanoid commercial contract.

specialized.note: Deploying across all 42 Walmart regional distribution centers; acquired Walmart's Advanced Systems and Robotics business in 2025 with a 400-system, $5B+ multi-year backlog.

humanoid.note: Apollo is operating in designated zones at GXO Logistics warehouses and Mercedes-Benz / Jabil factories; raised $520M at a $5B valuation in February 2026 with 2026 commercial-scale targets.

specialized.note: LG Electronics took a 51% controlling stake in Bear Robotics in January 2025 at a reported ~$600M valuation; Servi runs across thousands of restaurants, hotels, and senior-living sites in the U.S., Korea, and Japan.

Neo robots are deployed across five continents at Singapore Changi, Paris CDG, and Cincinnati airports plus DHL warehouses and major shopping malls; Tennant + Brain Corp's competing X6 ROVR is rolling out fleetwide in 2026.

humanoid.note: 1X is shipping early NEO units into homes in late 2025 / early 2026 with substantial teleoperator support, positioned around tidying, surface wiping, and laundry. Physical Intelligence's π0 laundry-folding demo is the strongest pure-autonomy result.

specialized.note: Commercial driverless Class 8 service launched on the Dallas–Houston lane in May 2025 with Uber Freight and Hirschbach as paying customers; expanded to Fort Worth–El Paso in 2025 and connecting to Phoenix in early 2026, with 250,000 driverless miles logged.

Nearly 2,000 Spot robots deployed across customer sites (BMW, Cargill, power generation, construction), having inspected over 130,000 industrial assets; expanded from equipment-only inspection to full-facility coverage in 2025.

humanoid.note: Electric Atlas demos through 2025–2026 cover whole-body manipulation, tool and parts handling at Hyundai facilities, and dynamic locomotion useful for ladder and stair access — the most credible humanoid progress on facility-style physical tasks. No general-maintenance customer yet.

specialized.note: Over 75,000 UR cobots deployed worldwide with roughly 40% global cobot market share; the AI-ready UR15 launched at Automate 2025 cuts assembly cycle times by up to 30%.

humanoid.note: Figure 02 completed an 11-month BMW Spartanburg pilot, loading 90,000+ sheet-metal parts into ~30,000 X3s before retiring in November 2025; Figure 03 began shipping in late 2025 with monthly volume roughly doubling toward ~240 units by April 2026.

specialized.note: Roughly 20 Infinite Kitchen restaurants live by late 2025; Sweetgreen plans Infinite Kitchen in 75%+ of new builds in 2026 and sold the Spyce robotics arm to Wonder for ~$186M. Chef Robotics serves a parallel B2B prepared-meals niche (40M+ meals at Amy's Kitchen, Sunbasket, Cafe Spice).

humanoid.note: Published demos cover meal-prep manipulation: cracking eggs, handling produce, stovetop and cookware interaction, and kitchen cleanup, with π0.5 generalizing to unseen kitchens. Sunday Robotics targets the same space with fewer published technical results.

specialized.note: Nearly 100 Moxi units operate across 25+ U.S. hospitals (including Medical City Dallas), completing 1.25M+ deliveries and 100,000+ autonomous elevator rides. Serve Robotics announced acquisition of Diligent in January 2026; Moxi handles logistics, not nursing tasks.

humanoid.note: 1X has positioned NEO toward eldercare and in-home assistance — fetching, light support, companionship — and is the most visible humanoid in homes as of early 2026, though heavily teleoperated. No humanoid has a credible patient-handling clinical pilot.
